documents: [Fix for SEC-509-CERT-2] Fixed X.509 certificate chain validation in CAPF. The ssl_verify_cert() function only checked the leaf certificate. Updated to call X509_verify_cert() with the full chain store. Added OCSP stapling check and CRL distribution point lookup. Files changed: capf/src/ssl_utils.c, capf/src/cert_validator.c
[Comment on SEC-509-CERT-2] Root cause: X509_STORE was initialized without loading intermediate CAs. Fix: Load CA bundle from /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt into the store before verify.
[Comment on SEC-509-CERT-2] Also found that OCSP response was not being checked. Added ocsp_check() call after chain verify. See capf/src/ocsp_handler.c for the new function.
[Comment on SEC-509-CERT-2] Regression test added: test_cert_chain_validation() in capf/tests/test_ssl.py — covers self-signed, expired, revoked, and valid chain scenarios.

[Fix for SEC-TLS-VER-1] Disabled TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 on CallManager SIP TLS listener. Set minimum protocol version to TLS 1.2. Updated cipher suite list to only include AEAD ciphers. Verified with openssl s_client that TLS 1.0/1.1 connections are rejected.
[Comment on SEC-TLS-VER-1] Changed SSL_CTX_set_min_proto_version(ctx, TLS1_2_VERSION) in sip_tls.c. Also updated the cipher string to 'ECDHE+AESGCM:ECDHE+CHACHA20:DHE+AESGCM'.
[Comment on SEC-TLS-VER-1] Verified: openssl s_client -connect localhost:5061 -tls1 now fails with 'protocol version' error.

[Fix for SEC-AUT-PWD-1] Removed default admin credentials from platformConfig.xml. Implemented first-login forced password change flow. Added CI check (check_default_creds.py) to prevent default credentials from being committed.
[Comment on SEC-AUT-PWD-1] The default admin/admin was in platformConfig.xml line 42. Removed and replaced with a placeholder that triggers setup wizard on first boot.
[Comment on SEC-AUT-PWD-1] Added pre-commit hook: scripts/check_default_creds.py scans for common default credential patterns in config files.

[Code diff for SEC-AUT-PWD-1] --- a/cm/config/platformConfig.xml
+++ b/cm/config/platformConfig.xml
@@ -40,4 +40,4 @@
-    <admin-user>admin</admin-user>
-    <admin-password>admin</admin-password>
+    <admin-user>SETUP_REQUIRED</admin-user>
+    <admin-password>CHANGE_ON_FIRST_LOGIN</admin-password>
[Fix for SEC-HRD-SSH-1] Hardened sshd_config on CAPF server. Disabled root login, disabled password authentication, set key-based auth only. Added banner and reduced MaxAuthTries to 3.

[Comment on SEC-HRD-SSH-1] Changes to /etc/ssh/sshd_config:
PermitRootLogin no
PasswordAuthentication no
PubkeyAuthentication yes
MaxAuthTries 3
Banner /etc/ssh/banner.txt
[Comment on SEC-HRD-SSH-1] Tested: SSH as root now returns 'Permission denied'. Key-based login works for service account.

[Code diff for SEC-HRD-SSH-1] --- a/install/templates/sshd_config
+++ b/install/templates/sshd_config
@@ -12,4 +12,6 @@
-PermitRootLogin yes
-PasswordAuthentication yes
+PermitRootLogin no
+PasswordAuthentication no
+PubkeyAuthentication yes
+MaxAuthTries 3

[Fix for SEC-DEP-LIB-1] Upgraded log4j from 1.2.17 to 2.21.0 in XCP module to remediate CVE-2021-44228. Migrated all log4j 1.x API calls to log4j 2.x API. Converted log4j.xml config to log4j2.xml format.
[Comment on SEC-DEP-LIB-1] Dependency chain: log4j-core 2.21.0, log4j-api 2.21.0, log4j-slf4j2-impl 2.21.0. Removed log4j 1.2.17 from pom.xml.
[Comment on SEC-DEP-LIB-1] Import changes: org.apache.log4j.Logger → org.apache.logging.log4j.LogManager. Logger.getLogger(cls) → LogManager.getLogger(cls).
[Comment on SEC-DEP-LIB-1] Config migration: log4j.xml appenders converted to log4j2.xml syntax. PatternLayout format preserved.

[Code diff for SEC-DEP-LIB-1] --- a/xcp/pom.xml
+++ b/xcp/pom.xml
@@ -55,4 +55,8 @@
-    <dependency><groupId>log4j</groupId><artifactId>log4j</artifactId><version>1.2.17</version></dependency>
+    <dependency><groupId>org.apache.logging.log4j</groupId><artifactId>log4j-core</artifactId><version>2.21.0</version></dependency>
+    <dependency><groupId>org.apache.logging.log4j</groupId><artifactId>log4j-api</artifactId><version>2.21.0</version></dependency>

[Fix for SEC-NET-PORT-1] Changed DRF backup service bind address from 0.0.0.0 to 127.0.0.1. The service only needs local access for backup operations. Added iptables rule to drop external traffic on port 4040.
[Comment on SEC-NET-PORT-1] In drf_backup.conf: bind_address changed from 0.0.0.0 to 127.0.0.1. Also added iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 4040 -j DROP to firewall rules.
[Comment on SEC-NET-PORT-1] Verified with 'netstat -tlnp | grep 4040' — now shows 127.0.0.1:4040 instead of 0.0.0.0:4040.

[Code diff for SEC-NET-PORT-1] --- a/drf/config/drf_backup.conf
+++ b/drf/config/drf_backup.conf
@@ -8,2 +8,2 @@
-bind_address=0.0.0.0
+bind_address=127.0.0.1

[Fix for SEC-LOG-AUD-1] Converted Unity authentication logging from plain text to CEF format. Created cef_log_auth_event() utility function. All auth success/failure events now emit CEF-formatted syslog messages.
[Comment on SEC-LOG-AUD-1] CEF format: CEF:0|Cisco|Unity|14.0|AUTH_SUCCESS|User Login|3|src=x dst=y suser=admin
[Comment on SEC-LOG-AUD-1] Replaced 12 instances of log_auth_event() with cef_log_auth_event() across 4 files.

[Code diff for SEC-LOG-AUD-1] --- a/unity/src/auth_logger.py
+++ b/unity/src/auth_logger.py
@@ -22,4 +22,8 @@
-def log_auth_event(user, success):
-    logger.info(f'Auth {"success" if success else "failure"} for {user}')
+def cef_log_auth_event(user, success, src_ip, dst_ip):
+    severity = 3 if success else 7
+    event = 'AUTH_SUCCESS' if success else 'AUTH_FAILURE'
+    cef = f'CEF:0|Cisco|Unity|14.0|{event}|User Login|{severity}|src={src_ip} dst={dst_ip} suser={user}'
+    syslog.info(cef)

[Fix for SEC-509-CERT-2] Fixed X.509 certificate chain validation in CAPF. The ssl_verify_cert() function only checked the leaf certificate. [Comment on SEC-509-CERT-2] Root cause: X509_STORE was initialized without loading intermediate CAs. Fix: Load CA bundle from /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt into the store before verify.
[Comment on SEC-509-CERT-2] Also found that OCSP response was not being checked. Added ocsp_check() call after chain verify. See capf/src/ocsp_handler.c for the new function.
[Comment on SEC-509-CERT-2] Regression test added: test_cert_chain_validation() in capf/tests/test_ssl.py — covers self-signed, expired, revoked, and valid chain scenarios.
